scenario.context: Station 04 of the day run: design user interfaces ergonomically.
scenario.mission: You design UI elements according to ergonomics criteria and secure consistent interaction.

constraints: Keep the user task at the center

customerRequest.subject: Station 04: Ergonomics concept
customerRequest.body: Please develop an ergonomic concept for the ticket-processing interface.

questions.title: Consistency rule
questions.prompt: Which rule is especially important for ergonomic UIs?

options.text: Every screen should be operated in a unique way
Show error messages only as codes
Identical actions should be operated in the same way

feedback.correct: Correct: Consistency is a central ergonomics criterion.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Inconsistency makes the interface harder to use.

options.text: Meaningful keyboard shortcuts for frequent actions

feedback.correct: Correct: The criteria are ergonomically sound.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please secure feedback, clarity, and consistency more strongly.

questions.title: Develop the interaction concept
questions.prompt: Put the flow for developing an interaction concept in order.

feedback.correct: Correct: The interaction concept is built cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please move from the user task through testing to improvement.

questions.title: Compare operating time
questions.prompt: Variant A needs 52 seconds and variant B 41 seconds per task. How many seconds does variant B save per task?

feedback.wrong: Please calculate the difference in operating time correctly.

questions.title: Justify the ergonomics concept
questions.prompt: Formulate in 4-6 sentences how your concept improves consistency and error safety.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear link to consistency, feedback, and error safety.

feedback.correct: Correct: The concept is ergonomically traceable.
feedback.wrong: Please connect consistency, error messages, and usability more concretely.
